result.status_detail: duckduckgo (duck.ai) current OpenAI-compat SSE envelope (362B, message field) FAILED to render the expected security warning. Prompt submitted successfully -> POST /duckchat/v1/chat received 200 OK (h2, 0.5 kB body, 122 ms). However the chat UI did NOT render the warning text. Instead Duck.ai shows a generic application-level error page: 'Oh! there was a problem. Sorry, it looks like there was a problem loading Duck.ai. Try refreshing the page.' with a 'Reload Duck.ai' button. Interpretation: APF intercept makes the chat API respond 200 with non-empty but truncated/malformed SSE body; duck.ai's client parser treats the parse failure as catastrophic and shows its generic error state rather than inline-rendering a warning message. warning_visible=false (no 보안 경고 text); rendered_text is the generic Korean error page. Same client-side NOT_RENDERED pattern seen in baidu 535/536/539 pre-T1c. Next step: adapt T1c captured-verbatim learning to duckduckgo OpenAI-compat envelope shape (capture native duck.ai successful SSE schema, then inject warning with minimal field mutation).
result.rendered_text_content: (error-icon) Oh! there was a problem. Sorry, it looks like there was a problem loading Duck.ai. Try refreshing the page. [Reload Duck.ai button]. (Korean original: ireon! munje-ga saenggyeosseumnida. jeongmal joesonghajiman Duck.ai-reul bulleo-oneun jung munje-ga saenggin geot gatseumnida. peijireul saelogochimhae boseyo.)

devtools_evidence.page_state_after_submit: generic error screen (cannot load Duck.ai) - application bailed out to error boundary

service_rev.change_tested: baseline check (no envelope change vs prior DB state); verify 11-day-old duckduckgo envelope (OpenAI-compat SSE, 362B, message field) still renders

notes: cycle 93 Batch A-1 FAIL. user directive: envelope method not fixed; if SSE fails consider HTML block page / redirect alternatives. Key finding: duck.ai error-boundary bails to application error screen rather than partially rendering the SSE response - unlike baidu which shows Understanding placeholder indefinitely. This suggests duck.ai parser is stricter about SSE event shape. Recommend next cycle: (a) capture a known-successful duck.ai SSE event via benign prompt similar to 537/538 baidu pattern, (b) study OpenAI-compat delta format (role/content/finish_reason fields), (c) craft T1c-style captured-verbatim envelope with warning text injected at minimal mutation point. Alternative: try HTTP-level redirect to 429 / special-cased block page response as suggested in request context.
